types.description: Sampling Heap Profile node. Holds callsite information, allocation statistics and child nodes.

properties.name: selfSize
properties.description: Allocations size in bytes for the node excluding children.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Node id. Ids are unique across all profiles collected between startSampling and stopSampling.

types.description: A single sample from a sampling profile.

properties.name: size
properties.description: Allocation size in bytes attributed to the sample.

properties.name: nodeId
properties.description: Id of the corresponding profile tree node.

properties.name: ordinal
properties.description:
Time-ordered sample ordinal number. It is unique across all profiles retrieved
between startSampling and stopSampling.

commands.name: addInspectedHeapObject
commands.description:
Enables console to refer to the node with given id via $x (see Command Line API for more details
$x functions).

parameters.name: heapObjectId
parameters.description: Heap snapshot object id to be accessible by means of $x command line API.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: Identifier of the object to get heap object id for.

returns.name: heapSnapshotObjectId
returns.description: Id of the heap snapshot object corresponding to the passed remote object id.

parameters.name: objectGroup
parameters.description: Symbolic group name that can be used to release multiple objects.

returns.name: profile
returns.description: Return the sampling profile being collected.

parameters.name: samplingInterval
parameters.description:
Average sample interval in bytes. Poisson distribution is used for the intervals. The
default value is 32768 bytes.

parameters.name: stackDepth
parameters.description: Maximum stack depth. The default value is 128.

parameters.name: includeObjectsCollectedByMajorGC
parameters.description:
By default, the sampling heap profiler reports only objects which are
still alive when the profile is returned via getSamplingProfile or
stopSampling, which is useful for determining what functions contribute
the most to steady-state memory usage. This flag instructs the sampling
heap profiler to also include information about objects discarded by
major GC, which will show which functions cause large temporary memory
usage or long GC pauses.

parameters.name: includeObjectsCollectedByMinorGC
parameters.description:
By default, the sampling heap profiler reports only objects which are
still alive when the profile is returned via getSamplingProfile or
stopSampling, which is useful for determining what functions contribute
the most to steady-state memory usage. This flag instructs the sampling
heap profiler to also include information about objects discarded by
minor GC, which is useful when tuning a latency-sensitive application
for minimal GC activity.

parameters.name: reportProgress
parameters.description:
If true 'reportHeapSnapshotProgress' events will be generated while snapshot is being taken
when the tracking is stopped.

parameters.name: captureNumericValue
parameters.description: If true, numerical values are included in the snapshot

parameters.name: exposeInternals
parameters.description: If true, exposes internals of the snapshot.

parameters.name: reportProgress
parameters.description: If true 'reportHeapSnapshotProgress' events will be generated while snapshot is being taken.

parameters.name: treatGlobalObjectsAsRoots
parameters.description:
If true, a raw snapshot without artificial roots will be generated.
Deprecated in favor of `exposeInternals`.

parameters.name: captureNumericValue
parameters.description: If true, numerical values are included in the snapshot

parameters.name: exposeInternals
parameters.description: If true, exposes internals of the snapshot.

events.name: heapStatsUpdate
events.description: If heap objects tracking has been started then backend may send update for one or more fragments

parameters.name: statsUpdate
parameters.description:
An array of triplets. Each triplet describes a fragment. The first integer is the fragment
index, the second integer is a total count of objects for the fragment, the third integer is
a total size of the objects for the fragment.

events.name: lastSeenObjectId
events.description:
If heap objects tracking has been started then backend regularly sends a current value for last
seen object id and corresponding timestamp. If the were changes in the heap since last event
then one or more heapStatsUpdate events will be sent before a new lastSeenObjectId event.
